metadata.summary: Voice market context: adoption stats and platform comparison for strategic selection.

content.title: VUI Market Context
content.summary: Voice market context: adoption stats and platform comparison for strategic decision-making.

why.p: Platform selection decisions and stakeholder primers require concrete, sourced adoption figures, not anecdote. The methodology provides a canonical stat schema and a refresh pattern so agents can pull current numbers on demand rather than relying on stale hardcoded values. LLM-native voice agents (ChatGPT Voice, Claude voice) have reshaped the landscape since 2024 and now constitute a fifth platform category bypassing skill stores.

list.item: Strategy phase of a voice-product proposal: ground the deck in current adoption stats and platform tradeoffs.
Platform-selection decision: choose Alexa vs Google Assistant vs Siri vs custom LLM-VUI based on developer surface and user reach in target geos.
Quarterly brief refresh: market data ages fast; agents pull current numbers on demand instead of relying on stale README values.
Investor / stakeholder primers: a one-page market context written from canonical sources (Statista, Voicebot.ai, NNg, Gartner).
Implementation work — this methodology is descriptive market context, not how-to.
Single-vendor decisions already locked — re-justification is not useful.
Real-time competitive intelligence — use a market-research methodology with monitoring loops instead.

rule.statement: Every market statistic must carry four fields: value, year, source URL, and geographic scope. Stats without scope or year are not citable.
rule.rationale: Voice adoption varies 3-5x between US and global figures. A "62% use voice assistants" claim without geographic scope is meaningless and misleads strategy decisions.

rule.statement: Distinguish these three denominator categories: smart-speaker households, voice-assistant users, voice-search query share. These are not interchangeable and come from different measurement methodologies.
rule.rationale: Mixing denominators produces false comparisons. Smart-speaker household penetration tracks device ownership; voice-assistant users tracks intent sessions; voice-search share tracks query-type on a specific engine. Conflating them inflates or deflates the picture arbitrarily.

section.name: current-baseline
section.p: Reference figures as of 2025-2026 (verify and refresh via refresh-script.py before use): 8.4B+ voice assistants in use globally; 20%+ of internet queries use voice search; 62% of US adults use a voice assistant; 55% of US households own a smart speaker; 71% of users prefer voice over typing for some tasks. All figures: US/global mixed — verify geo before reusing.

rule.statement: Stamp every market brief with a refreshed_at date. Refuse to publish if the brief is older than 90 days. Voice adoption statistics moved sharply post-2024 with LLM-native voice agents.
rule.rationale: LLM-native voice agents (ChatGPT Voice, Claude voice mode) are reshaping the landscape faster than canonical 2024-25 sources track. Stats older than 90 days may be wrong on direction, not just magnitude.

section.reference: Python script to refresh market stats via web search

list.item: Statista — primary canonical source for voice adoption stats (some behind paywall)
Voicebot.ai — voice industry tracking, free annual surveys
Edison Research / Infinite Dial — US adoption baselines, annual PDF
Nielsen Norman Group — UX-focused voice interaction analysis
Pew Research — US technology adoption baselines (free)
Gartner — hype cycle and enterprise predictions (paywalled)

rule.statement: Restrict web-search queries to the trusted-source list. Reject SEO content-farm listicles ("10 voice assistant statistics") that cite each other without primary sources.
rule.rationale: LLMs default to ranking high-traffic listicles, which are recycled secondary aggregations. Requiring the agent to return primary-source URLs eliminates circular citation.

section.name: platform-landscape
section.p: Five platforms as of 2025-2026: Alexa (Amazon devices, smart home — developer access via Alexa Skills Kit); Google Assistant (Android, Nest, web — Actions on Google; note: Conversational Actions deprecated 2023); Siri (Apple ecosystem — SiriKit, restricted intent set, requires native iOS app); Bixby (Samsung devices — Bixby Developer Studio, small reach outside Korea); Custom LLM-VUI (any platform via API — OpenAI Realtime, Claude voice, ElevenLabs Conversational — bypasses skill stores and certification entirely).

rule.statement: Weight platform reach by target geo, not global share. Alexa dominates US smart speakers; Google Assistant dominates Android phones globally; Siri dominates iOS. These rankings invert across geos.
rule.rationale: A platform that reaches 40% of US smart-speaker households reaches a very different population than one reaching 40% of Android phones in APAC. Geo-specific reach is the decision-relevant variable, not headline global figures.

rule.statement: Treat LLM-native voice agents as a fifth platform category, not a feature. They bypass skill stores and certification, support open-domain dialog, and have different design requirements (no intent/slot NLU, state managed by model or tool use).
rule.rationale: LLM-native voice agents (Pipecat, Vapi, Retell, Claude voice) represent a different architectural paradigm from intent-based platforms. Conflating them with Alexa/Google design patterns produces broken systems: intent/slot design applied to open LLM dialog, or unconstrained LLM dialog applied where deterministic state is required.

rule.statement: For platform selection: (1) identify target geos and primary user device type; (2) check developer SDK health (commit activity, deprecation notices); (3) assess LLM integration openness; (4) evaluate certification requirements against timeline. Document the tradeoff, not just the winner.
rule.rationale: Platform decisions are long-term bets. A platform with better current reach but a deprecated SDK or a shrinking developer community may be the wrong choice for a two-year product horizon.

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: This methodology is reference content, not a workflow. Drive it as: a research agent fetches the latest stats from named sources via WebSearch + WebFetch, normalizes them into the same schema as the existing README, and produces a delta report ("3 numbers changed since last brief"). A strategy agent then assembles the platform-comparison section keyed to the user's target geos, devices, and language coverage.

list.item: faion-market-researcher (from pro/research/market-researcher) — pulls and normalizes market stats.
faion-product-manager — translates market context into a platform-selection recommendation.
faion-gtm-strategist (from pro/marketing/gtm-strategist) — folds adoption stats into go-to-market positioning.
faion-ux-researcher-agent — connects market patterns to user-research priorities (which segments, which contexts).

section.name: prompt-patterns
section.prompt: Find the most recent (2025-2026) figures for: {metric_list}. For each, return: value, year, source URL, geographic scope. Reject sources older than 18 months unless no newer source exists. Output as JSON.
Compare {platforms} on: developer SDK quality, supported locales, monetization, user reach in {target_geos}, openness to LLM integration. Output a markdown table + a 3-sentence recommendation.

list.item: gh (GitHub CLI) — Inspect platform SDK repos for activity signals. brew install gh.
Anthropic SDK + WebSearch tool — Live source-grounded refresh. https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/agents-and-tools/tool-use/web-search-tool
jq — Parse market-data JSON exports. distro package.
markdown-it / pandoc — Render briefs to PDF/HTML for stakeholders. brew install pandoc.
Statista (SaaS, partial agent-friendly) — Most behind paywall. Primary canonical source for voice adoption stats.
Voicebot.ai (SaaS free reports, yes agent-friendly) — Tracks voice industry; free annual surveys.
eMarketer / Insider Intelligence (SaaS, partial agent-friendly) — Paywalled. Forecasts, demographic breakdowns.
Gartner (SaaS, partial agent-friendly) — Paywalled. Hype cycle, predictions.
Edison Research (Public PDFs) — "Smart Audio Report" (now Infinite Dial).
Pew Research (Free, yes) — US adoption baselines.
Alexa Skills Kit (SaaS, yes agent-friendly) — Full SDK. Ecosystem comparison input.
Google Actions (SaaS, yes agent-friendly; some surfaces deprecated) — Note: Google deprecated Conversational Actions in 2023.
SiriKit (SaaS, limited agent-friendly) — Restricted intent set. Requires native iOS app; no third-party voice apps.
Bixby Developer Studio (SaaS, yes) — Samsung-only; small reach outside Korea.
OpenAI Realtime API / ElevenLabs Conversational (SaaS, yes APIs) — Custom LLM-VUI route bypassing platform constraints.

section.name: template-and-scripts
section.p: This is a reference doc; the relevant template is the comparison table in the README. Minimal refresh helper:
section.code: # refresh_vui_brief.py import anthropic, json, datetime, pathlib METRICS = [ "voice assistants in use globally", "% US adults using voice assistants", "% households with smart speakers", "voice search share of internet queries", ] client = anthropic.Anthropic() msg = client.messages.create( model="claude-opus-4-7", max_tokens=2000, tools=[{"type":"web_search_20250305","name":"web_search"}], messages=[{"role":"user","content": f"For each metric, return latest 2025-2026 figure with source URL: {METRICS}. " "JSON list of {metric, value, year, source_url, geo}."}], ) out = {"refreshed_at": datetime.date.today().isoformat(), "data": json.loads(msg.content[-1].text)} pathlib.Path("vui_market_brief.json").write_text(json.dumps(out, indent=2))

list.item: Always cite the source year and geo with every stat; "62% use voice assistants" without scope is uselessly vague.
Compare apples to apples: distinguish "smart speaker households" from "voice-assistant users" from "voice-search query share".
Refresh quarterly minimum; voice adoption moved sharply post-LLM (ChatGPT Voice, Pi, Claude voice). Stats older than 12 months are likely wrong on direction, not just magnitude.

For platform selection, weight reach by *target geo*, not global. Alexa dominates US smart speakers; Google Assistant dominates Android phones globally; Siri dominates iOS; none of these flips a wrong-platform decision.
Treat LLM-native voice agents as a fifth platform, not a feature — they bypass skill stores and certification entirely.
LLMs hallucinate plausible-looking statistics with fake source URLs. Require the agent to return source URL + access date and verify URLs resolve.

WebSearch results often surface SEO content farms (top results = "10 voice assistant statistics" listicles citing each other). Restrict trusted-source list explicitly: Statista, NNg, Gartner, Edison/NPR, Pew, Voicebot.
Stats expressed in different units (devices vs users vs households) get mixed in synthesis. Normalize to a single denominator before comparison.
Market briefs go stale silently. Stamp every brief with `refreshed_at` and refuse to publish if older than 90 days.
Geographic scope mismatch: a "US adoption" stat used as "global" inflates by 3-5x. Always validate geo before reusing.

template.purpose: Python script: calls Claude with web_search tool to refresh stat list; stamps refreshed_at date.

checklist.check: All market statistics carry four fields: value, year, source URL, geographic scope.
Distinguish between smart-speaker households, voice-assistant users, and voice-search query share.
Market brief is stamped with refreshed_at date.
Brief is less than 90 days old.
Platform reach is weighted by target geo, not global share.
LLM-native voice agents are treated as a distinct platform category.
